title: The Best & Worst Supplements to Improve Sleep

chunks.title: Introduction to Sleep Troubleshooting
chunks.summary: The video starts by emphasizing the importance of following basic practices for improving sleep quality, such as maintaining a consistent sleep schedule and avoiding stimulants before bedtime. It suggests that these foundational steps might resolve sleep issues for most people.
chunks.transcript: in terms of how to troubleshoot your poor sleep if you are doing all the right things then we have to actually have the conversation about troubleshooting your sleep but do the right things first avoid blue light go to sleep at the same time get up at the same time get sunlight in the eyes in the morning get grounding get grounding throughout the day consider a grounding mat turn off your Wi-Fi at night eat good food don't eat it too close to sleeping avoid caffeine avoid alcohol avoid marijuana avoid blue light avoid Avid stimulating conversations avoid um television avoid Instagram at late at night do all those things first before you even think about anything else to improve your sleep so if you're doing all of those things and you're still not sleeping well then it's time to have the conversations about what might help your sleep but remember I think for maybe 80 90% doing those things will improve your sleep and probably fix your sleep issues but you have to do those things beyond

chunks.title: Role of Diet in Sleep Quality
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses how diet influences sleep, particularly highlighting the benefits of carbohydrates. They share personal insights on how adding carbs to a ketogenic diet can enhance sleep quality and stress that some people may misinterpret their sleep needs due to an overactive sympathetic nervous system.
chunks.transcript: that obviously I think diet is a huge Factor getting the right nutrients getting enough protein getting carbohydrates is critical for sleep I think so many in the keto Community would have better sleep when they added carbohydrates to their diet I certainly found this in the keto Community sometimes people will say oh I feel like I need less sleep well yeah that's because your sympathetic nervous system is overactive that is your fight ORF flight nervous system it's overactive it's a stress hormone your catac colomines are up that's why you feel like you need less sleep but you don't you still need sleep it's just your body is stressed your cortisol is up so I don't think you need less sleep with less carbohydrates as I've talked about many times in the past carbohydrates are beneficial for humans I'm very clear on this not necessarily all carbohydrates but you guys know what I mean often times there's some confusion around

chunks.title: Magnesium and Sleep
chunks.summary: This segment covers the mixed evidence surrounding magnesium supplements and their effectiveness for sleep. The speaker indicates that while magnesium can be beneficial, it is readily available in animal-based foods, and emphasizes the importance of dietary sources over supplements.

chunks.title: Concerns with Melatonin
chunks.summary: The video highlights the speaker's skepticism regarding melatonin supplements, citing potential negative effects observed in animal studies. They recommend allowing the body to produce melatonin naturally by avoiding blue light at night instead of relying on external supplements.
chunks.transcript: separate conversation something I've talked about in the past with regard to phytic acid Keating all sorts of minerals selenium iron magnesium all the divalent cat ions calcium Etc so magnesium maybe melatonin I'm not a fan of melatonin at all doses on the shelves in stores are massively super physiologic and I think this could have major consequences for humans there's good evidence that in animal models giving Mega doses of melatonin shrinks the gonads so you tell me how much Melatonin you want to take guys I don't want to take it um I've taken it I think maybe twice in my life when I was in medical school who knows if it worked or it was placebo effect I don't take melatonin I don't think it's valuable for humans let your body make it by not

chunks.title: Supplement Alternatives for Sleep
chunks.summary: The speaker briefly discusses other substances that people use to aid sleep, such as tart cherry, kiwi, and GABA derivatives. They express caution about the habit-forming nature of GABA-related products and warn against relying on pharmaceuticals that may negatively impact sleep quality.
chunks.transcript: blocking your rhythms with blue light at night there are some other studies on tarte Cherry and kiwi for sleep I'm not convinced either of those are terribly useful but see what helps you some people have used other things to relax them at night personally I have found that the grounding mat is perhaps the best relaxation I've found at night so see if that helps you but there aren't any great supplements for sleep there are some Gaba gamma amino beyc acid derivatives that some people have tried be aware that anything with Gaba may be

chunks.title: Effort and Lifestyle Changes for Better Sleep
chunks.summary: Wrapping up, the speaker stresses that improving sleep requires genuine effort and lifestyle modifications rather than seeking quick fixes. They advocate for a long-term approach to sleep health, emphasizing that mindfulness and commitment to change can yield significant benefits.
chunks.transcript: habit forming this is benzodiazapines alcohol all of these things can affect Gaba it's a dangerous slope I'm not a fan of things like Ambi known as ZM or Lunesta known as a Zopa clone often these have rebound they do affect your Sleep Quality negatively by decreasing REM sleep so there are no shortcuts I think when it comes to sleep you have to do the work it's like meditating you you really should just do the work and I think that most of us will sleep much better if we actually just simply put an effort to it and make the lifestyle changes that we should be making in the first place and that will pay off significantly in the long run
